title: Reduce workspace defaults runtime delegate

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Determine whether defaults.report can reduce its generated Python runtime delegate without moving workspace policy judgment into command-generation.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep scope bounded to defaults.report and the generated Python operation/runtime ownership proof. Do not hand-edit generated output.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Replace generic deterministic defaults behavior with codegen-owned/resource-backed primitives if it is data-only; otherwise close this slice as no-safe-route with evidence.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A correct fix requires extracting the entire workspace defaults policy payload into declarative package-owned data or changing command-generation ownership boundaries.
canonical_core.next_action: Audit defaults payload ownership, then either implement a resource-backed generated load primitive or record the no-safe-route result.
completion_criteria: defaults.report runtime delegate reduction is either implemented through source contracts and regeneration, or explicitly closed as no-safe-route because the current payload is package policy assembly rather than generic deterministic primitive work.
